journal_entry.reason: Ford acquires land with an existing building for $60,000,000 payable in 6 annual instalments of $10,000,000 each, starting at year-end. Under IFRS, the asset and liability are recorded at present value using the market rate of 6%. The existing building will be demolished immediately; under IAS 16.16(b) and 16.20, costs to prepare land for its intended use (including demolition) are capitalized into the Land account. PV calculation: PV = sum of [10,000,000 / (1.06)^i for i=1 to 6] = 10,000,000/1.06 + 10,000,000/1.06^2 + 10,000,000/1.06^3 + 10,000,000/1.06^4 + 10,000,000/1.06^5 + 10,000,000/1.06^6 = 9,433,962 + 8,899,964 + 8,396,193 + 7,920,937 + 7,472,583 + 7,049,606 = 49,173,245. The Land is recorded at this present value, and the liability (Long-term borrowings or Notes payable) is recorded at the same amount. The difference between face value ($60M) and PV ($49.17M) will be recognized as interest expense over the payment period.

output_debit_classifier.reason: Ford acquires land with an existing building for the purpose of constructing a new factory. The stated purchase price is $60,000,000, payable in 6 equal annual instalments of $10,000,000 each, with the first payment due at the end of the year of acquisition. Under IFRS (IAS 16), when payment is deferred beyond normal credit terms, the asset and liability must be recorded at present value using the market interest rate. The market rate is stated as 6%. The present value of the 6 annual payments is calculated as: PV = 10,000,000/1.06 + 10,000,000/1.06^2 + 10,000,000/1.06^3 + 10,000,000/1.06^4 + 10,000,000/1.06^5 + 10,000,000/1.06^6 = 9,433,962 + 8,899,964 + 8,396,193 + 7,920,937 + 7,472,583 + 7,049,606 = 49,173,245. The existing building will be demolished immediately. Under IAS 16.16(b) and 16.20, costs directly attributable to bringing an asset to the location and condition necessary for its intended use are capitalized. Since the land is acquired for factory construction and the existing building must be removed, the demolition cost is part of preparing the land for its intended use and is capitalized into the Land account (not expensed). The transaction text states the purchase includes both land and building for a single price, so the entire present value is allocated to Land (the building has no separate value as it will be demolished). The liability is recorded at the same present value. The $10,826,755 difference between the face value ($60M) and present value ($49.17M) represents implicit interest that will be recognized as interest expense over the 6-year payment period using the effective interest method.
